column1: # When dispatchFnDef runs an fnq body it pushes the matched args (in
# sig order) onto the registry's argsStack. The 0-arg `argsq` word
# returns that frame as a List, so a body can read positional argsq
# by index without naming each param.
# The frame pops automatically when the body returns (via the
# try/finally in dispatchFnDef), and `argsq` outside any fnq call
# Single-arg fnq — body uses args lengthq / first.
defq f1 ( fnq [ a:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ argsq lengthq ] ) 5 f1
defq f2 ( fnq [ a:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ argsq firstq ] ) 7 f2
# Two-arg fnq — args list reflects sig order.
defq f3 ( fnq [ a:Integer b:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ argsq lengthq ] ) 3 f3 4
defq f4 ( fnq [ a:Integer b:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ argsq firstq ] ) 3 f4 4
column3: sig[0] = forward arg (4) — same as a

column1: defq f5 ( fnq [ a:Integer b:Integer c:Integer ] [ Integer ] [ argsq lengthq ] ) 1 2 3 f5
column3: all-stack call form; args still has 3 entries
